column7: Typo – Please note that correct transliteration instills the correct "understanding", among students etc. This may be considered as a Global Correction.
Typo – Please note that correct transliteration instills the correct "understanding", among students etc. This may be considered as a Global Correction.

Typo – Headword Print Change!  The actual Sanskrit word meaning "the wrist" is kalkuṣa (कल्कुष) or, more traditionally in its Vedic feminine dual form, kalkuṣī (कल्कुषी).    LB Textual Source: The word appears originally in the Śatapatha Brāhmaṇa (10.2.6.14). LB Commentarial Exegesis: In his commentary on this passage, the prominent scholar Sāyaṇa explicitly glosses the term as: LB kalkuṣī = maṇibandhāratnī (मणिबन्धारत्नी) LB Meaning: The wrist (maṇibandha) and the forearm/elbow (aratni).    LB How the Error Occurred LB The mistake is purely orthographic and paleographic, arising from a misreading of hand-written manuscripts or an early printing press typo: LB In the Devanagari script, the conjunct consonant cluster ल्कु (-lku-) as found in कल्कुष looks remarkably similar to ल्यु (-lyu-) if the loop of the ka is slightly compressed or faint. LB An early lexicographer misread the true form kalkuṣa (कल्कुष) as kalyuṣa (कल्युष), copy-pasting the correct definition ("The wrist") onto a non-existent word.

column6: Please Add the following Five Compounds as indicated in the window display: " ˚jña , ˚kara , ˚kālaḥ , ˚prāṇa , ˚mātra ". According to the AP spelling, these compounds will appear as: "kiñcijjña, kiñcitkara, kiñcitkāla, kiñcitprāṇa, kiñcinmātra". These Five Compounds Deserve to be listed as Searchable Headwords within the Hierarchy, in order to Fully Honor and Pay Resect to AP57.

column7: Typo – Headword Print Change !  The uttarapada "stanuḥ" is a lexicographical corruption or typographical error preserved in Apte’s dictionary. The true uttarapada of this compound is "tanu", meaning "body" or "slender," and the correct word is kiṃtanuḥ! Both Yates and Wilson record the word correctly as kintanu.

column6: Please Add the following Compound as indicated in the window display: " ˚īśvaraḥ  ". According to the AP spelling, this compound will appear as: "kimpuruṣeśvaraḥ". This Compound Deserves to be listed as Searchable Headwords within the Hierarchy, in order to Fully Honor and Pay Resect to AP57.
